title: Tap center of circle to pause/resume sweeping
description:
On the main screen, tapping the middle of the circular Mbps readout should pause (and presumably resume) the live sweeping/measurement. Right now pausing does not appear to be possible or supported from that central control.

User intent / why it matters: Scout is used like a live signal hunter — you sweep the phone around a room watching the big central Mbps number. When you find a good spot, you want to freeze the reading in place so you can look at it without it continuing to update. A tap-to-pause gesture directly on the number you're already staring at is the natural, thumb-free way to do that mid-sweep, rather than reaching for a separate start/stop control.

Context for conversion (from CLAUDE.md + app design): the app currently has a start/stop control (its own accessibilityIdentifier) that begins/ends measurement. The central readout is the big Mbps figure (updated ~4x/sec) inside a circular UI element. This idea is about adding a pause/resume affordance to the CENTRAL circle itself (tap the number to freeze it), distinct from the existing start/stop button. Open for conversion to decide: whether 'pause' means freezing the displayed value while the sampler keeps running vs. actually suspending the transfer; how it relates to / differs from the existing start/stop control; the resume gesture; and any paused-state visual indication + accessibility identifier/label. Not resolving those here.
